So plants that have the same market, they buy the same feedstock, they produce the same product into the same market, same access to people, capital, technology, same regulations, same compliance, same everything.
And some of them fail, some of them succeed.
And we've seen over quite a bit of assessments that we've done over workshops, like over more than 200 workshops and dozens of plants with the right personas.
We've seen a pattern, a pattern that separates the winners and the losers.
Okay, and that pattern is the ability of the winners to define and master something called coordinated operating strategies and execute those strategies rigorously.
Okay.
What are these strategies?
Basically,
They're an area of the plant, an aspect of the plant.
It's not necessarily one physical area.
It's not necessarily one physical product being generated.
But it's some aspect of the plant where they want to run it in a way that is safe, reliable, that maximizes their margin, energy compliance, and all that.
And when they have it, when they run it well, they're all able to focus on their goals, have clear KPIs, their execution is measurable.
They're able to adapt the plant to market conditions.
So if you need to produce something, change and pivot as the market behaves, you're able to do it quickly.
And if you're a customer, we can actually tell you what these things are.
It's not some kind of magic.
It's things that are pretty simple.
